lessons.title: Play the Italian Game
lessons.description: Learn the Italian Game opening moves and understand the plans behind each move.

steps.instruction: Start with 1.e4 - control the center and open lines for your Bishop and Queen!

feedback.correct: Great! 1.e4 controls d5 and f5, and opens the diagonal for the Bishop.
feedback.incorrect: Play e4 - the most popular opening move, controlling the center.

steps.instruction: Black mirrors you with ...e5. Now develop with 2.Nf3 - attack Black's e5 pawn while developing!

feedback.correct: Perfect! The Knight develops to a great square, attacking e5 and controlling d4.
feedback.incorrect: Play Nf3 - develop with a threat! The Knight attacks the e5 pawn.

steps.instruction: Black defends with ...Nc6. Now play 3.Bc4 - the Italian Game! Target the weak f7 square.

feedback.correct: The Italian Game! Your Bishop aims at f7, the weakest point near Black's King.
feedback.incorrect: Play Bc4 to enter the Italian Game. The Bishop targets f7!

steps.instruction: Black develops symmetrically. Now castle with O-O to keep your King safe!

feedback.correct: Excellent! King safety first. Your King is safe and the Rook is connected.
feedback.incorrect: Castle kingside (O-O) - get your King to safety early!

steps.instruction: Now play d3 - support your e4 pawn and prepare to develop your Bishop.

feedback.correct: Solid! d3 supports e4, opens the diagonal for Bc1, and keeps a strong center.
feedback.incorrect: Play d3 - a solid move that supports your center and opens development.

lessons.successMessage: You've learned the Italian Game! Key ideas: control center, develop pieces, target f7, castle early.
hints: The Italian Game starts with 1.e4 e5 2.Nf3 Nc6 3.Bc4
Always aim the Bishop at f7 - it's Black's weakest point
Castle early to protect your King

lessons.title: Play the London System
lessons.description: Learn the London System - a solid opening system for White that works against almost anything.

steps.instruction: Start with 1.d4 - control the center with your d-pawn!

feedback.correct: Good! 1.d4 is the foundation of the London System.
feedback.incorrect: Play d4 - the London System always starts with this move.

steps.instruction: Now the key London move: 2.Bf4! Develop the Bishop BEFORE playing e3.

feedback.correct: The signature London move! The Bishop is developed before e3 blocks it in.
feedback.incorrect: Play Bf4 - this is THE London System move. Develop the Bishop before e3!

steps.instruction: Now play 3.e3 - support d4 and prepare to develop the kingside Bishop.

feedback.correct: Solid! e3 supports d4 and prepares Bd3 and kingside castling.
feedback.incorrect: Play e3 - now that the Bishop is out, e3 is safe.

steps.instruction: Develop the Bishop with Bd3 - a natural square pointing at the Black kingside.

feedback.correct: The London setup is taking shape! Bd3 eyes the kingside.
feedback.incorrect: Play Bd3 - complete the London formation.

steps.instruction: Support the center with c3 - anchor your d4 pawn!

feedback.correct: The London pyramid is complete: d4, e3, c3 with Bf4 and Bd3. A rock-solid setup!
feedback.incorrect: Play c3 - this supports d4 and completes the London formation.

lessons.successMessage: You've learned the London System! Setup: d4, Bf4, e3, Bd3, c3, then castle and play for the center.
hints: This system works against almost any Black defense

lessons.title: Defend Against Scholar's Mate
lessons.description: Learn to spot and punish the Scholar's Mate attempt (Qh5/Bc4 attack on f7).

steps.instruction: White is threatening Qxf7#! How do you defend f7 while developing a piece?

feedback.correct: Well defended! You've blocked the mate threat while developing.
feedback.incorrect: Look for a move that defends f7. Qe7 blocks the diagonal, or Nh6 guards f7.

lessons.successMessage: You know how to defend against Scholar's Mate! Remember: never panic, just develop and defend.
hints: White wants to play Qxf7# - you must defend f7
Qe7 blocks the diagonal while developing the Queen
After defending, White's Queen is misplaced early

lessons.title: Best Developing Move
lessons.description: Choose the best developing move following opening principles.

steps.instruction: You played 1.e4, Black played 1...Nc6. What's the best developing move? Develop a piece with a purpose!

feedback.correct: Excellent choice! Either Nf3 (developing toward the center) or d4 (claiming more center space) are principled moves.
feedback.incorrect: Follow opening principles: develop pieces toward the center. Nf3 or d4 are best.

lessons.successMessage: Great opening instincts! Always develop with purpose: control center, develop pieces, prepare castling.
hints: Opening principles: develop pieces toward the center
